subject: Nickel
executive_summary:
Nickel occupies a unique position among critical minerals as a commodity where a single nation -- Indonesia -- dominates global production at approximately 60% of world supply (2.2 million tonnes out of 3.7 million tonnes globally in 2024), while the United States has only one operating primary nickel mine, Eagle Mine in Michigan, producing approximately 8,000 tonnes in 2024 with net import reliance of 48%. The supply landscape is undergoing significant disruption as Indonesia has slashed its national nickel mining quota by 120 million tons for 2025 (from 272 million to 150 million tons), with 2026 ceilings set at 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million in 2025.

Nickel is critical for both defense and clean energy applications. Nickel-based superalloys are essential for jet engine components operating at temperatures exceeding 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit, while high-nickel cathode chemistries are increasingly important for long-range electric vehicle batteries. China consumes over 63% of primary nickel globally, and the International Energy Agency projects nickel demand will more than double by 2035 to over 5.5 million tonnes.

The U.S. domestic production outlook is precarious. Eagle Mine was slated for closure around 2026 before Talon Metals completed its acquisition and announced plans to extend mine life while advancing the Tamarack Nickel-Copper Project in Minnesota and Beulah Battery Minerals Processing Facility. The DOD has awarded $20.6 million to support nickel prospecting in Minnesota and Michigan. Price dynamics remain challenging -- Goldman Sachs raised their 2026 forecast to $17,200 per tonne, but oversupply from Indonesian processing expansion (particularly Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate) may keep prices depressed in the low-$15,000s range, threatening the economic viability of higher-cost Western mining projects.

key_findings.headline: Indonesia controls 60% of global nickel supply and is cutting quotas sharply
key_findings.detail: Indonesia produced approximately 2.2 million tonnes of nickel content in 2024, nearly 60% of the global 3.7 million tonne total. The government slashed the 2025 mining quota by 120 million tons (a 44% cut from 272 to 150 million tons) and set 2026 ceilings at 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million. This represents a deliberate policy to preserve reserves and push downstream value-added processing domestically.

key_findings.headline: U.S. has only one primary nickel mine, facing closure without life extension
key_findings.detail: Eagle Mine in Michigan is the sole primary nickel mine in the United States, producing approximately 8,000 tonnes in 2024 with net import reliance of 48%. The mine was slated for closure around 2026 before Talon Metals acquired it from Lundin Mining. Talon plans to extend mine life through exploration and advance the Tamarack project in Minnesota, but new production capacity remains years away.

key_findings.headline: Nickel superalloys are irreplaceable in jet engines and military applications
key_findings.detail: Nickel-based superalloys maintain structural integrity at temperatures exceeding 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit, making them essential for jet engine turbine blades, combustion chambers, and other hot-section components. There are no practical substitutes for these applications. Military modernization programs worldwide are increasing demand for these high-performance materials.

key_findings.headline: China consumes 63% of global primary nickel, creating demand concentration risk
key_findings.detail: China held 63.5% of global nickel demand in 2025, primarily for stainless steel production and growing battery cathode manufacturing. This demand concentration, combined with Indonesia's dominant supply position, creates a China-Indonesia axis that controls the majority of the global nickel value chain from mine to battery.

key_findings.headline: DOD awards $20.6 million for nickel prospecting in Minnesota and Michigan
key_findings.detail: The Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to developers of the proposed Talon nickel mine in Minnesota under the critical minerals supply chain program, with funds supporting prospecting work in Michigan and Minnesota to identify new domestic nickel resources.

key_findings.headline: Indonesia's MHP surge threatens to keep nickel prices depressed
key_findings.detail: Indonesia's Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP) output is projected to reach 482,000 tonnes of nickel metal equivalent in 2025, a nearly 50% rise from 2024. This surge in intermediate processing capacity, combined with broader supply expansion, may keep nickel prices in the low-$15,000s per tonne range through 2026, potentially below the cost of production for many non-Indonesian mines.

recent_developments.headline: Talon Metals completes acquisition of Eagle Mine, launches ambitious 2026 exploration
recent_developments.summary: Talon Metals Corp completed its acquisition of the Eagle Mine and Humboldt Mill from Lundin Mining, adding the only primary nickel mine in the United States to its portfolio. Talon announced it will execute its most ambitious exploration program to date in 2026, targeting Eagle Mine life extension, regional exploration, Tamarack project permitting, and feasibility advancement.
recent_developments.impact: Secures continued operation of the only U.S. primary nickel mine and creates a pathway for expanded domestic production, though new capacity remains years away.

recent_developments.headline: Indonesia slashes 2025 nickel mining quota by 44%
recent_developments.summary: Indonesia reduced its national nickel mining quota from 272 million tons in 2024 to 150 million tons for 2025, a 120 million ton cut representing a 44% reduction. The government cited reserve preservation, downstream processing prioritization, and environmental compliance as motivations. For 2026, the quota was set at 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million in 2025.
recent_developments.impact: Tightens global nickel ore markets and may trigger price rebounds, while accelerating Indonesia's transition from raw ore exports to processed nickel products.

recent_developments.headline: DOD awards $20.6 million for domestic nickel prospecting
recent_developments.summary: The Department of Defense awarded $20.6 million to support nickel prospecting in Minnesota and Michigan, funding the Talon nickel project as part of the critical minerals domestic supply chain strengthening program.
recent_developments.impact: Provides federal support for domestic nickel resource identification, though converting exploration to production requires 7-15 years.

recent_developments.headline: Goldman Sachs raises 2026 nickel price forecast by 16%
recent_developments.summary: Goldman Sachs raised its 2026 nickel price forecast by 16% to an average of $17,200 per tonne, driven by Indonesia's production cuts and growing EV demand. However, competing analyses suggest oversupply from Indonesian processing expansion could keep prices in the low-$15,000s range.
recent_developments.impact: Price uncertainty creates investment risk for Western nickel projects that typically require higher prices to be economically viable.

recent_developments.headline: U.S. tariffs create additional uncertainty for nickel supply chains
recent_developments.summary: The sharp rise in U.S. tariffs in spring 2025 and resulting trade tensions significantly impacted the metals and minerals sector. Nickel market growth forecasts were reduced due to tariff impacts between the U.S. and trading partners, adding another layer of uncertainty to already volatile supply chains.
recent_developments.impact: Tariffs increase costs for U.S. manufacturers dependent on imported nickel while doing little to stimulate near-term domestic production.

risk_assessment.current_risk: Nickel supply chain risk is high and growing. The U.S. has a single operating mine producing 8,000 tonnes against significant defense and industrial needs, with 48% import reliance. Indonesia's dominance at 60% of global supply, combined with aggressive quota cuts and China's 63% demand share, creates a concentrated supply-demand axis largely outside U.S. influence. The potential closure of Eagle Mine without life extension would eliminate all domestic primary nickel production.
key_drivers: Indonesia's 44% quota cut for 2025 and further reductions in 2026 tightening ore supply
Eagle Mine was facing 2026 closure before Talon acquisition; life extension uncertain
China consumes 63% of global primary nickel, controlling downstream demand
Indonesia's MHP production surge may keep prices below viability for Western mines
IEA projects nickel demand more than doubling by 2035 to over 5.5 Mt
U.S. tariffs adding cost uncertainty without stimulating domestic production
LFP battery chemistry growth potentially reduces battery nickel demand growth rate
